Home

Attract, Retain and Grow

Making Engineering and Physical Sciences research a domain for all in the North of England

End of Project Workshop – 26th May 2021

The Northern Power team are delighted to invite you to our project workshop. Live scribed by Laura Evans of Nifty Fox Productions, this promises to be an inspiring and informative day, designed to assist Early Career Researchers in their personal development, and to support HEI and Industry in retaining and developing diverse communities of people within their organisations.

In this free, one day, online workshop we share what we did, findings from our EDI in STEM research and listen to inspiring speakers and panels. With networking and development sessions for early career participants, and sessions relevant to EDI, HR and Organisation Development professionals, this promises to be a day with something for everyone.

Read more

The Northern Power Inclusion Matters project was funded by the Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council (EPSRC). The project helped shape an actively inclusive culture in the Engineering and Physical Sciences (EPS) community in the North of England. The aim was to, drive and sustain greater equality for all, including people of different genders, ethnicity, sexual orientation, and disabilities.

Project Activities

– Shared Characteristics Mentoring
– Reciprocal Mentoring
– Industry Collaboration
– Networking and Leadership
– Evaluation
– Online Platform

Outcomes for E & I Practices

– How-to guides
– Templates
– Policy briefs
– Lessons learnt

Outcomes for Personal Development

– Career development
– E&I support
– Personal development
